name: tuple
title: Tuple
description: Browse and call contacts, control your active call, and search transcripts from your Tuple pair programming sessions.
author: stephendolan

commands.name: search-contacts
commands.title: Search Contacts
commands.description: Browse your contacts and start a call.

commands.name: active-call
commands.title: Active Call
commands.description: Show and control your active call from the menu bar.

commands.name: search-calls
commands.title: Search Calls
commands.description: Browse recent calls, search what was said, and ask AI about them.

commands.name: search-rooms
commands.title: Search Rooms
commands.description: Browse your personal and team rooms, see who's in them, and join.

commands.name: join-personal-room
commands.title: Join Personal Room
commands.description: Join your most recent personal room.

commands.name: auto-title-summary
commands.title: Generate Title & Summary
commands.description: Generate and save a title & summary for your most recent call with AI — applied immediately, no review.

commands.name: toggle-mute
commands.title: Toggle Mute
commands.description: Mute or unmute your microphone in the active call.

tools.name: list-recent-calls
tools.title: List Recent Calls
tools.description: List recent recorded Tuple calls (most recent first), optionally filtered by participant.

tools.name: search-transcripts
tools.title: Search Transcripts
tools.description: Full-text search across all stored call transcripts; returns matching spoken segments.

tools.name: read-transcript
tools.title: Read Transcript
tools.description: Read the full speaker-attributed transcript of one recorded call.

tools.name: list-contacts
tools.title: List Contacts
tools.description: List Tuple contacts with online status, favorite, and recent flags.

tools.name: get-active-call
tools.title: Get Active Call
tools.description: Report whether the user is on a call right now, with whom, and their mute state.

tools.name: list-rooms
tools.title: List Rooms
tools.description: List personal and team Tuple rooms and who is currently in each.

ai.instructions: Calls are recorded Tuple pair-programming sessions stored locally. The tools are read-only. To answer questions about what was said, use search-transcripts with a few focused key words or a name (all terms must appear in a segment, so keep queries specific; search again with different terms to broaden). For recency or 'who/when' questions, use list-recent-calls (it accepts a participant filter and includes any saved summary). Resolve a person to their email with list-contacts when needed; contacts carry an online/busy/offline status, so use it for presence questions like 'who is around right now?' (busy means in a call). Rooms are persistent named spaces distinct from calls and can be occupied even when the user isn't on a call — use list-rooms for 'who is in a room?' or 'which rooms are active?'. Use read-transcript only when you need a call's full text. Cite the call (participants and date) in answers.

evals.input: @tuple am I on a call right now?
@tuple when did I last talk with Jordan?
@tuple what did we say about the migration?
@tuple is anyone in a room right now?

preferences.name: tuplePath
preferences.title: Tuple CLI Path
preferences.description: Path to the tuple CLI executable. Leave blank to auto-detect (/opt/homebrew/bin then /usr/local/bin); set this only if you installed it elsewhere.

preferences.name: exportDirectory
preferences.title: Transcript Export Folder
preferences.description: Where exported transcripts are saved. Defaults to your Downloads folder.
